items.summary: President Donald Trump tapped Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., earlier this month to replace Kristi Noem.

items.headline: How America’s war is turning into a European market nightmare
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran are hurting European markets most of all, with interest-rate expectations surging for both the Bank of England and European Central Bank on Thursday.

items.headline: European Central Bank holds rates steady, warns outlook is 'significantly more uncertain'
items.summary: The war in Iran has upset the economic equilibrium Europe threatening energy supplies, growth and the outlook for consumer prices, upsetting economic forecasts.

items.headline: Alibaba’s stock slips as a big drop in net income overshadows AI progress
items.summary: While Alibaba’s cloud and AI units delivered another quarter of triple-digit growth, the company’s costly pivot to rapid e-commerce delivery led to an earnings miss.

items.headline: Micron falls 5% after blowout earnings. CEO says it can supply only a fraction of key customer needs
items.summary: Micron stock is up more than 350% in the past year, however, thanks to a memory supply shortage driven by surging demand for Nvidia's AI chips.

items.headline: ‘Epic Fury’ has already canceled out Big Beautiful Bill’s tax refunds — even if the Iran war ended today
items.summary: A group of Stanford economists pit tax refunds versus rising gasoline prices — and the results aren’t good for American households.

items.headline: Gulf states have tolerated Iranian strikes so far — but their 'defensive' stance won't last forever
items.summary: The Gulf states have said Iran's attacks on energy infrastructure won't "go unanswered" but, so far, they have not hit back. That stance could change soon.

items.headline: Trump warns U.S. will 'blow up' South Pars gas field in Iran if strikes against Qatar energy continue
items.summary: World leaders are scrambling to contain a spiraling Middle East conflict on Thursday after Israel and Iran traded strikes on gas production facilities.

items.headline: Hegseth says potential $200 billion Iran war spending request could shift: 'Takes money to kill bad guys'

items.headline: Is the economy really losing jobs? The low number of unemployment filings says no.
items.summary: Is the U.S. economy really losing jobs, as the February employment report found? Not according to the low number of people applying for jobless benefits.

items.headline: HSBC’s CFO said the bank would turn to AI to cut costs. Now the bank is reportedly consider 20,000 job cuts.

items.summary: Lilly is betting big on retatrutide as the next pillar of its obesity portfolio after its weight loss injection Zepbound and its upcoming pill, orforglipron.
